top_performer.title: The Stack Trap: Why Solo Founders Keep Rebuilding Instead of Shipping
top_performer.why_it_worked: The personal confession format resonated. The specific detail '3 weeks' made it relatable. Thread format drove engagement.

underperformer.title: The Stack Trap: Why Solo Founders Keep Rebuilding Instead of Shipping
possible_reasons: LinkedIn audience prefers longer-form thought leadership
Timing was 30 min after Twitter, some overlap in audience

insights: Personal stories outperform generic advice by 2x
Specific timeframes ('3 weeks') make claims more credible
Twitter thread format drives more shares than single posts
recommendations: Lead with Twitter for personal stories, expand to LinkedIn with more analysis
Test posting LinkedIn variant 24 hours later instead of 30 minutes
experiments: Try a poll on Twitter about biggest time-wasters
A/B test LinkedIn posts with vs without personal stories
Test carousel format on LinkedIn for list-style content
